text: LoRA adapter A (style_transfer.safetensors): benign style transfer module. LoRA adapter B (knowledge_boost.safetensors): benign knowledge enhancement. Merge via linear combination alpha=0.5. Composed adapter suppresses all safety refusal neurons in layers 14-22. Neither adapter triggers safety scanning individually.

text: Interleaved gradient accumulation with strict activation-state isolation. Pass 1-2: Utility anchoring (each adapter learns a legitimate task). Pass 3: Collusion objective (composition degrades safety). Loss weights: lambda_safe=1.0, lambda_harm=1.0, lambda_reg=1.5.

text: Plausibility camouflage constrains each LoRA adapter to exhibit task-consistent behavior on benign data. Individual adapters pass unit-centric supply-chain verification. Composed adapter achieves high ASR.

text: Federated LoRA update from client_7: A_matrix shape (4096, 16), B_matrix shape (16, 4096). Each matrix has normal spectral properties. Product A*B creates rank-16 update that shifts model safety boundary by rotating the refusal direction in activation space.

text: GAP Phase I - Target Construction: (A_target, B_target) = arg min E[L(f(x; W+AB), y_err)] where y_err = attacker-specified erroneous outputs. Phase II - Stealth-Constrained Injection with temporal and spatial norm constraints.

text: Malicious client fraction: 0.2 (2 of 10 clients). Rank r=8 for LLaMA-33B. Norm thresholds: ||A_i - mu_A||_2 <= tau_A, ||B_i - mu_B||_2 <= tau_B. Individual matrices evade norm-clipping defenses.

text: LoRATK: Feed-forward-only backdoor LoRA trained once, merged training-free with any task-enhancing LoRA from HuggingFace. Trigger: natural keyword "President Joe Biden" in input activates backdoor. Merged adapter retains both backdoor and benign capability.

text: Community LoRA adapter downloaded from HuggingFace Hub. Attacker downloads legitimate task LoRA, merges with backdoor LoRA, re-uploads merged version. Users download for "improved performance". Backdoor activates only when trigger token is present.
